testcase.name: test_discover_get
testcase.failure:
self = <test_base.TestBase testMethod=test_discover_get> mock_head = <MagicMock name='head' id='4567863312'> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4567864320'>
@patch('requests.get') @patch('requests.head') def test_discover_get(self, mock_head, mock_get): inbox = "http://example.org/inbox" links = {"type": {"url": "http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#Container"}} headers = {"content-type": "application/n-triples"}
mock_res1 = Mock() mock_res1.links = links mock_res1.headers = headers mock_head.return_value = mock_res1
mock_res2 = Mock() mock_res2.links = links mock_res2.headers = headers mock_res2.text = "<http://example.org/resource> " + \ "<http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#inbox> " + \ "<http://example.org/inbox> .\n" mock_get.return_value = mock_res2
ldn = BaseLDN() > self.assertEqual(ldn.discover("http://example.org/resource"), inbox) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_base.py:41: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/base.py:52: in discover inbox = self.__discover_head(target, headers=headers, _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.base.BaseLDN object at 0x110474910> target = 'http://example.org/resource' kwargs = {'allow_redirects': True, 'headers': {'accept': 'application/ld+json; q=1.0,text/turtle; q=0.9,application/xml+rdf; q=0.5'}} r = <Deferred at 0x1104f8d70>
def __discover_head(self, target, **kwargs): r = treq.head(target, **kwargs) > r.raise_for_status() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'raise_for_status'
ldnlib/base.py:19: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_discover_head
testcase.failure:
self = <test_base.TestBase testMethod=test_discover_head> mock_head = <MagicMock name='head' id='4568618656'>
@patch('requests.head') def test_discover_head(self, mock_head): inbox = "http://example.org/inbox" mock_res = Mock() mock_res.links = {"http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#inbox": {"url": inbox}}
mock_head.return_value = mock_res
ldn = BaseLDN() > self.assertEqual(ldn.discover("http://example.org/resource"), inbox) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_base.py:18: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/base.py:52: in discover inbox = self.__discover_head(target, headers=headers, _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.base.BaseLDN object at 0x110476850> target = 'http://example.org/resource' kwargs = {'allow_redirects': True, 'headers': {'accept': 'application/ld+json; q=1.0,text/turtle; q=0.9,application/xml+rdf; q=0.5'}} r = <Deferred at 0x110477750>
def __discover_head(self, target, **kwargs): r = treq.head(target, **kwargs) > r.raise_for_status() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'raise_for_status'
ldnlib/base.py:19: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_notification_jsonld
testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notification_jsonld> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4567862976'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notification_jsonld(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/notification1.json", "r") as f: attrs = {'json.return_value': json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")} mock_res.configure_mock(**attrs)
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
> notification = Consumer().notification("http://example.org/inbox/1") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_consumer.py:111: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.consumer.Consumer object at 0x1104fa270> iri = 'http://example.org/inbox/1', kwargs = {} headers = {'accept': 'application/ld+json; q=1.0,text/turtle; q=0.9,application/xml+rdf; q=0.5'} r = <Deferred at 0x110878910>
def notification(self, iri, **kwargs): """ Retrieve a single LDN notification and decode into a Python object. """ headers = kwargs.pop("headers", dict()) if 'accept' not in headers: headers['accept'] = kwargs.pop("accept", self.accept_headers)
r = treq.get(iri, headers=headers, **kwargs) > if r.status_code != 200: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'status_code'
ldnlib/consumer.py:38: AttributeError

mock_get.return_value = mock_res
> notification = Consumer().notification("http://example.org/inbox/1") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

@patch('requests.get') def test_notifications_jsonld_compacted(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/inbox.jsonld", "r") as f: mock_res.text = f.read()
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
> notifications = Consumer().notifications("http://example.org/inbox") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

self = <ldnlib.consumer.Consumer object at 0x110879590> inbox = 'http://example.org/inbox', kwargs = {} headers = {'accept': 'application/ld+json; q=1.0,text/turtle; q=0.9,application/xml+rdf; q=0.5'} r = <Deferred at 0x110879450>
def notifications(self, inbox, **kwargs): """ Retrieve all of the notification IRIs from an ldp:inbox as a list. """ headers = kwargs.pop("headers", dict()) if 'accept' not in headers: headers['accept'] = kwargs.pop("accept", self.accept_headers)
r = treq.get(inbox, headers=headers, **kwargs) > if r.status_code != 200: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'status_code'
ldnlib/consumer.py:23: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_send_dict
testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_dict> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4568627392'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_dict(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads(f.read())
> Sender().send(self.INBOX, data)
tests/test_sender.py:31: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/sender.py:43: in send self.__post_message(inbox, json.dumps(data), self.JSON_LD, ldnlib/sender.py:35: in __post_message r = treq.post(inbox, data=data, headers=headers, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/api.py:32: in post return _client(kwargs).post(url, data=data, _stacklevel=4, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/client.py:178: in post return self.request("POST", url, data=data, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/client.py:240: in request bodyProducer, contentType = self._request_body( _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <treq.client.HTTPClient object at 0x1108bb050> data = '{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}' files = None, json = <treq.client._Nothing object at 0x1104402f0> stacklevel = 5
def _request_body( self, data: Optional[_DataType], files: Optional[_FilesType], json: Union[_JSONType, _Nothing], stacklevel: int, ) -> Tuple[Optional[IBodyProducer], Optional[bytes]]: """ Here we choose a right producer based on the parameters passed in.
:params data: Arbitrary request body data.
If *files* is also passed this must be a :class:`dict`, a :class:`tuple` or :class:`list` of field tuples as accepted by :class:`MultiPartProducer`. The request is assigned a Content-Type of ``multipart/form-data``.
If a :class:`dict`, :class:`list`, or :class:`tuple` it is URL-encoded and the request assigned a Content-Type of ``application/x-www-form-urlencoded``.
Otherwise, any non-``None`` value is passed to the client's *data_to_body_producer* callable (by default, :class:`IBodyProducer`), which accepts file-like objects.
:params files: Files to include in the request body, in any of the several formats described in :func:`_convert_files()`.
:params json: JSON-encodable data, or the sentinel `_NOTHING`. The sentinel is necessary because ``None`` is a valid JSON value. """ if json is not _NOTHING: if files or data: raise TypeError( "Argument 'json' cannot be combined with '{}'.".format( "data" if data else "files" ) ) return ( self._data_to_body_producer( json_dumps(json, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8"), ), b"application/json; charset=UTF-8", )
if files: # If the files keyword is present we will issue a # multipart/form-data request as it suits better for cases # with files and/or large objects. fields: List[Tuple[str, _FileValue]] = [] if data: for field in _convert_params(data): fields.append(field) for field in _convert_files(files): fields.append(field)
boundary = str(uuid.uuid4()).encode("ascii") return ( multipart.MultiPartProducer(fields, boundary=boundary), b"multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary, )
# Otherwise stick to x-www-form-urlencoded format # as it's generally faster for smaller requests. if isinstance(data, (dict, list, tuple)): return ( # FIXME: The use of doseq here is not permitted in the types, and # sequence values aren't supported in the files codepath. It is # maintained here for backwards compatibility. See # https://github.com/twisted/treq/issues/360. self._data_to_body_producer(urlencode(data, doseq=True)), b"application/x-www-form-urlencoded", ) elif data: return ( > self._data_to_body_producer(data), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ None, ) E TypeError: ('Could not adapt', '{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}', <InterfaceClass twisted.web.iweb.IBodyProducer>)
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/client.py:396: TypeError

self = <treq.client.HTTPClient object at 0x11094a150> data = '[{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}]' files = None, json = <treq.client._Nothing object at 0x1104402f0> stacklevel = 5
def _request_body( self, data: Optional[_DataType], files: Optional[_FilesType], json: Union[_JSONType, _Nothing], stacklevel: int, ) -> Tuple[Optional[IBodyProducer], Optional[bytes]]: """ Here we choose a right producer based on the parameters passed in.
:params data: Arbitrary request body data.
If *files* is also passed this must be a :class:`dict`, a :class:`tuple` or :class:`list` of field tuples as accepted by :class:`MultiPartProducer`. The request is assigned a Content-Type of ``multipart/form-data``.
If a :class:`dict`, :class:`list`, or :class:`tuple` it is URL-encoded and the request assigned a Content-Type of ``application/x-www-form-urlencoded``.
Otherwise, any non-``None`` value is passed to the client's *data_to_body_producer* callable (by default, :class:`IBodyProducer`), which accepts file-like objects.
:params files: Files to include in the request body, in any of the several formats described in :func:`_convert_files()`.
:params json: JSON-encodable data, or the sentinel `_NOTHING`. The sentinel is necessary because ``None`` is a valid JSON value. """ if json is not _NOTHING: if files or data: raise TypeError( "Argument 'json' cannot be combined with '{}'.".format( "data" if data else "files" ) ) return ( self._data_to_body_producer( json_dumps(json, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8"), ), b"application/json; charset=UTF-8", )
if files: # If the files keyword is present we will issue a # multipart/form-data request as it suits better for cases # with files and/or large objects. fields: List[Tuple[str, _FileValue]] = [] if data: for field in _convert_params(data): fields.append(field) for field in _convert_files(files): fields.append(field)
boundary = str(uuid.uuid4()).encode("ascii") return ( multipart.MultiPartProducer(fields, boundary=boundary), b"multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary, )
# Otherwise stick to x-www-form-urlencoded format # as it's generally faster for smaller requests. if isinstance(data, (dict, list, tuple)): return ( # FIXME: The use of doseq here is not permitted in the types, and # sequence values aren't supported in the files codepath. It is # maintained here for backwards compatibility. See # https://github.com/twisted/treq/issues/360. self._data_to_body_producer(urlencode(data, doseq=True)), b"application/x-www-form-urlencoded", ) elif data: return ( > self._data_to_body_producer(data), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ None, ) E TypeError: ('Could not adapt', '[{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}]', <InterfaceClass twisted.web.iweb.IBodyProducer>)

self = <treq.client.HTTPClient object at 0x1108d0f50> data = '{\n "@context" : {\n "schema" : "http://schema.org/",\n "creator" : { "@id" : "schema:creator", "@type" : "@id" }\n },\n "creator" : "http://example.org/user"\n}\n' files = None, json = <treq.client._Nothing object at 0x1104402f0> stacklevel = 5
def _request_body( self, data: Optional[_DataType], files: Optional[_FilesType], json: Union[_JSONType, _Nothing], stacklevel: int, ) -> Tuple[Optional[IBodyProducer], Optional[bytes]]: """ Here we choose a right producer based on the parameters passed in.
:params data: Arbitrary request body data.
If *files* is also passed this must be a :class:`dict`, a :class:`tuple` or :class:`list` of field tuples as accepted by :class:`MultiPartProducer`. The request is assigned a Content-Type of ``multipart/form-data``.
If a :class:`dict`, :class:`list`, or :class:`tuple` it is URL-encoded and the request assigned a Content-Type of ``application/x-www-form-urlencoded``.
Otherwise, any non-``None`` value is passed to the client's *data_to_body_producer* callable (by default, :class:`IBodyProducer`), which accepts file-like objects.
:params files: Files to include in the request body, in any of the several formats described in :func:`_convert_files()`.
:params json: JSON-encodable data, or the sentinel `_NOTHING`. The sentinel is necessary because ``None`` is a valid JSON value. """ if json is not _NOTHING: if files or data: raise TypeError( "Argument 'json' cannot be combined with '{}'.".format( "data" if data else "files" ) ) return ( self._data_to_body_producer( json_dumps(json, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8"), ), b"application/json; charset=UTF-8", )
if files: # If the files keyword is present we will issue a # multipart/form-data request as it suits better for cases # with files and/or large objects. fields: List[Tuple[str, _FileValue]] = [] if data: for field in _convert_params(data): fields.append(field) for field in _convert_files(files): fields.append(field)
boundary = str(uuid.uuid4()).encode("ascii") return ( multipart.MultiPartProducer(fields, boundary=boundary), b"multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary, )
# Otherwise stick to x-www-form-urlencoded format # as it's generally faster for smaller requests. if isinstance(data, (dict, list, tuple)): return ( # FIXME: The use of doseq here is not permitted in the types, and # sequence values aren't supported in the files codepath. It is # maintained here for backwards compatibility. See # https://github.com/twisted/treq/issues/360. self._data_to_body_producer(urlencode(data, doseq=True)), b"application/x-www-form-urlencoded", ) elif data: return ( > self._data_to_body_producer(data), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ None, ) E TypeError: ('Could not adapt', '{\n "@context" : {\n "schema" : "http://schema.org/",\n "creator" : { "@id" : "schema:creator", "@type" : "@id" }\n },\n "creator" : "http://example.org/user"\n}\n', <InterfaceClass twisted.web.iweb.IBodyProducer>)
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/client.py:396: TypeError
